elements.sent-2411-001: A Sidon set $M$ is a subset of $\mathbb{F _2^t$ such that the sum of four distinct elements of $M$ is never 0.}
elements.sent-2411-002: The goal is to find Sidon sets of large size.
elements.sent-2411-003: In this note we show that the graphs of almost perfect nonlinear (APN) functions with high linearity can be used to construct large Sidon sets.

elements.sent-2411-009: In this note, we discuss only Sidon sets in $\binvn{t}$, the $t$-dimensional vector space over the binary field $\binf$.

elements.sent-2411-013: The trivial upper bound (already mentioned in \cite{BabaiSos1985 ) arises from the fact that the sums of two distinct elements of $M$ have to be distinct and non-zero, hence \begin{equation}\binom{\abs{M}}{2} = \frac{\abs{M}(\abs{M}-1)}{2} \leq \abs{\binvn{t}\setminzero}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-014: So far, progress on the upper bound has only been made in terms of coding theory.
elements.sent-2411-015: This uses a one-to-one correspondence between sum-free Sidon sets and linear codes with minimum distance $\geq 5$.
elements.sent-2411-016: A set in $\binvn{t}$ is called \mathKeyword{sum-free if the sum of any three elements of this set is different from 0.}
elements.sent-2411-017: If $M$ is a sum-free Sidon set, then $M\cup\set{0}$ is Sidon but not sum-free anymore.
elements.sent-2411-018: An overview of the one-to-one correspondence can be found in \cite{CzerwinskiPott2023 .}

elements.sent-2411-024: On the construction side, the following is known.
elements.sent-2411-025: In the case $t=2n$ even, Sidon sets of size $2^n+1$ are known.
elements.sent-2411-026: They are obtained from non-primitive BCH codes which are constructed as shortenings of codes with parameters $[2^n+1, 2^n+1-2n,5]$ (see \cite[page 586, 18.2 (ii)]{MacWilliamsSloane1978 or (C2) construction in \cite{Nagy2022arxiv ).}}
elements.sent-2411-027: Another construction is by Carlet, Mesnager and Picek \cite{CarletMesnager2022, CarletPicek2023 .}
elements.sent-2411-028: They have shown that the multiplicative subgroup $M$ of size $2^n+1$ in the finite field $\binfn{2n}$ is a Sidon set in the additive group of the field.

elements.sent-2411-165: The Walsh coefficients are the elements $\equiv 0 \bmod 4$ in the interval \begin{equation}[-2^{n/2+1}+1, 2^{n/2+1}+1],\end{equation} see \cite{LachaudWolfmann1999 , and therefore: \begin{thm The linearity of the inverse function equals to \begin{equation}\floor{2^{n/2+1}+1} - \left( \floor{2^{n/2+1}+1} \bmod 4\right)\end{equation} and leads to a Sidon set of size \begin{equation}2^{n-1} + \frac{1}{2}\Bigl(\floor{2^{n/2+1}+1} - \left( \floor{2^{n/2+1}+1} \bmod 4\right)\Bigr)\end{equation} in $\binvn{2n-1}$ for odd $n\geq 5$.. \end{thm.}}}

elements.sent-2411-173: A (binary) \mathKeyword{linear code $\code$ of \mathKeyword{length $m$ and \mathKeyword{dimension $k$ is a $k$-dimensional linear subspace $\code$ of $\binvn{m}$ and $c\in \code$ is called a \mathKeyword{code word of $\code$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-174: We consider all vectors to be row vectors.
elements.sent-2411-175: If the \mathKeyword{minimum distance of $\code$ is $d$, that is the minimum number of non-zero entries of all non-zero code words of $\code$, then $\code$ is called a $[m,k,d]$-code.}
elements.sent-2411-176: We call a $[m,m-t,d]$-code, a linear code of length $m$, with $t$ check bits and with minimum distance $d$.
elements.sent-2411-177: Such a $[m,m-t,d]$-code $\code$ can be defined as the kernel of a matrix $\checkMat$ with $t$ rows, $m$ columns and rank $t$ which is called parity-check matrix of $\code$, i.e $\code = \sett{v\in\binvn{n {\checkMat\cdot \transpose{v = 0}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-178: The one-to-one correspondence between sets and linear codes states that a sum-free Sidon set $M$ in $\binvn{t}$ with $\abs{M}\geq t+1$ and $\dim\vspan{M = t$ (that is the dimension of the linear span of $M$) leads to a linear code $\code_M$ of length $\abs{M}$, with $t$ check bits and with minimum distance $\geq 5$.}
elements.sent-2411-179: Thereby the $\abs{M}$ elements of $M$ form the columns of the parity-check matrix of $\code_M$.
elements.sent-2411-180: Since every Sidon set can be transformed into a sum-free Sidon set by translation and removing one element, we obtain: \begin{prop Every Sidon set $M$ in $\binvn{t}$ leads to a $[m-1,m-t-1,\geq 5]$-code.}
elements.sent-2411-181: In the following Theorem we summarise all the results of the previous section.. \begin{thm.}
elements.sent-2411-183: There exist $[m - 1, m - 2n - 2,5]$-codes for \begin{equation}m = 2^{n-1} + \frac{1}{2}\left(\floor{2^{n/2+1}+1} - \left( \floor{2^{n/2+1}+1} \bmod 4\right)\right)\end{equation} and odd $n\geq 5$.
